title: Java ArrayList
content:
### ArrayList in Java

ArrayList is a resizable array implementation of the List interface. It can dynamically grow and shrink.

---

### Creating an ArrayList

```java
import java.util.ArrayList;

ArrayList<String> names = new ArrayList<>();           // empty list
ArrayList<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>(20);       // initial capacity
ArrayList<String> copy = new ArrayList<>(otherList);    // from another collection
```

---

### Common Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `add(element)` | Add element to end |
| `add(index, element)` | Insert at specific index |
| `get(index)` | Get element at index |
| `set(index, element)` | Replace element |
| `remove(index)` | Remove by index |
| `remove(element)` | Remove by value |
| `size()` | Get number of elements |
| `contains(element)` | Check if element exists |
| `indexOf(element)` | Find index of element |
| `clear()` | Remove all elements |
| `isEmpty()` | Check if empty |

---

### Iterating

```java
// For loop
for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++) {
    System.out.println(list.get(i));
}

// For-each
for (String s : list) {
    System.out.println(s);
}
```

> **Key Point:** ArrayList maintains insertion order and allows duplicates. It grows by 50% when full. Use generic type parameter to specify element type. ArrayList is not synchronized (use Vector or Collections.synchronizedList for thread safety).

quiz.question: Can ArrayList hold primitive types directly?
What happens when ArrayList reaches its capacity?
